error_excerpt:

  --> src/yank.rs:32:5
   |
27 | #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   |          ----- in this derive macro expansion
...
32 |     tty_in: std::fs::File,
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ the trait `Clone` is not implemented for `File`

error[E0277]: the trait bound `File: Clone` is not satisfied
  --> src/yank.rs:33:5
   |
27 | #[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   |          ----- in this derive macro expansion
...
33 |     tty_out: std::fs::File,
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ the trait `Clone` is not implemented for `File`

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
   --> src/yank.rs:328:17
    |
327 |                 let field = &self.fields[i as usize];
    |                              ----------- immutable borrow occurs here
328 |                 self.twrite(&self.input_data[..field.so])?;
    |                 ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ mutable borrow occurs here
329 |                 self.tputs(T_ENTER_STANDOUT_MODE)?;
330 |                 self.twrite(&self.input_data[field.so..=field.eo])?;
    |                                              -------- immutable borrow later used here

error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*self` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
   --> src/yank.rs:329:17
    |
327 |                 let field = &self.fields[i as usize];
    |                              ----------- immutable borrow occurs here
328 |                 self.twrite(&self.input_data[..field.so])?;
329 |                 self.tputs(T_ENTER_STANDOUT_MODE)?;

diagnosis_plan.summary: The active batch is confined to src/yank.rs and appears to require only Rust-side ownership/borrowing repairs plus confirming whether TerminalState actually needs Clone semantics compared with the C/spec behavior.
diagnosis_plan.edit_strategy: First inspect all uses of TerminalState to determine whether Clone derive is unnecessary and should be removed, or whether a manual/semantic equivalent (such as file handle duplication) is actually required by usage and C/spec evidence. Then inspect tmain/twrite/tputs together and refactor the highlighted rendering block to avoid holding immutable borrows of self.fields/self.input_data across mutable method calls on self, likely by copying field indices/offsets into locals and/or narrowing borrows before each write. Do not create files or modules; this batch is localized to existing code.
reasoning: E0277 on std::fs::File almost always means a derived Clone is incompatible; before editing, we need to verify whether TerminalState is ever cloned or whether Clone can simply be removed without changing intended behavior.

E0502 indicates classic overlapping borrows in tmain: an immutable borrow of self.fields/self.input_data is kept alive while calling mutable methods on self, so reading the whole file and the helper method signatures is necessary to choose a borrow-shortening refactor.

Reading yank.c provides behavioral grounding for terminal state ownership and the rendering loop, ensuring the Rust fix preserves the original control flow rather than introducing ad hoc ownership workarounds.

Reading the behavior/spec files helps confirm whether terminal state duplication is part of the intended design or merely an accidental derive, and whether screen redraw semantics in tmain must match specific behavior.

updated_summary: Applied a minimal Rust-side ownership fix in src/yank.rs: TerminalState no longer derives Clone, and tmain now avoids overlapping immutable/mutable borrows during terminal writes by using local copied buffers. Recompile is needed to confirm whether this clears the active batch and reveal any remaining diagnostics.
structured_summary: Remove the unnecessary Clone derive from TerminalState and shorten immutable borrows in tmain by copying field offsets and cloning the display slices into local buffers before calling mutable terminal output methods.

edits.content:
#[derive(Debug)]
struct TerminalState {
    alt_screen: bool,
    rows: usize,
    cols: usize,
    tty_in: std::fs::File,
    tty_out: std::fs::File,
}

round_summary: Applied a minimal Rust-side ownership fix in src/yank.rs: TerminalState no longer derives Clone, and tmain now avoids overlapping immutable/mutable borrows during terminal writes by using local copied buffers. Recompile is needed to confirm whether this clears the active batch and reveal any remaining diagnostics.
handoff_summary_for_next_iteration:
This round fixed the blocking Clone error in src/yank.rs. TerminalState no longer derives Clone, which was effective because std::fs::File is not Clone. tmain was also adjusted to avoid overlapping immutable/mutable borrows during terminal output by copying needed buffer data into locals first; this did not introduce new blocking errors.

The result was accepted, so the baseline advanced to this updated project state. Current build output now gets past the previous hard errors and only shows warnings, including private interface visibility mismatches such as Yank::field_cmp exposing Field and Yank::tgetc likely exposing a too-private Key type.

Next round should avoid revisiting the removed Clone derive or reworking the borrow fix unless new evidence shows a regression. Prioritize cleaning up the remaining visibility/interface warnings in src/yank.rs and then re-run cargo check to uncover any further diagnostics beyond the truncated warning output.
